## Environment

- **Version**: 0.76.0
- **URL**: https://link-assistant.github.io/formal-ai/
- **Worker**: wasm worker
- **Mode**: manual

- **Diagnostics**: off
- **Timestamp**: 2026-05-20T10:52:56.793Z

## User Context

- **UI languages**: *en-US*, kk-KZ, kk, ru-RU, ru, de-DE, de, en
- **Theme**: auto (dark)
- **UI**: 411x775 viewport, 412x915 @2.625x screen, Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 10; K) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/148.0.0.0 Mobile Safari/537.36 browser, Android platform
- **Locale**: en-US (Asia/Almaty)
- **Guess probability**: 85%
- **Temperature**: 0.7
- **Follow-up probability**: 75%
- **Location**: inferred from time zone / locale only

## Dialog

Legend: `U` = user, `A` = agent.

```
U: Do you think space is continuous or discrete
A (intent: unknown): I do not have a learned symbolic rule for that prompt yet. Add a Links Notation fact or rule, then run the request again.
U: Do you think space is continuous or discrete
A (intent: unknown): I cannot answer that from local Links Notation rules yet. Please add a fact or add a rule in Links Notation, then run the request again.
U: Hi
A: Hey, how can I help?
U: Prove determinism the way logic can handle paradoxes like Godel's math incompleteness
A (intent: unknown, reported): I cannot answer that from local Links Notation rules yet. Please add a fact or add a rule in Links Notation, then run the request again.
```

## Reproduction Steps

1. Open https://link-assistant.github.io/formal-ai/
2. Send the prompt "Prove determinism the way logic can handle paradoxes like Godel's math incompleteness"
3. Click the report link on the dialog message

## Description

<!-- Please describe what looked wrong or incomplete. -->

## Attach full memory (optional)

Click **Export memory** in the topbar to save `formal-ai-memory.lino`, then attach it as a [GitHub Gist](https://gist.github.com) or wrap it in a `.zip` first. Redact sensitive content before uploading. See the [upload-memory guide](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/main/docs/upload-memory.md) for the full walkthrough.

comments.body:
For that we should use our own Rust prover as library from github.com/link-foundation/relative-meta-logic, and if it is not available as library, or has missing features to actually solve that, that need to be reported as issue.

In case of such cases, we should take math/logic/science as contexts, translate requirements to formal version of them using our formalization step, that involves wikidata and so on, make a formal plan of the proof, and execute on it by converting steps to actual expressions in github.com/link-foundation/relative-meta-logic, and we should provide our research result, as it should be either proven or disproven in the context of user requirements. Meaning each statement should be handled that way and formalized deeply using github.com/link-foundation/relative-meta-logic

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
